title: Fragrant Dutch-Oven British Frankfurter Bangers and Mash
description: A flavorful British dish featuring grassy, tender frankfurters, quickly cooked with nimono-simmer and served with creamy mash. Perfect for a weeknight dinner or a comforting meal.
instructions: Heat a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the butter and let it melt, then add the sliced onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute, stirring to prevent burning.
Add the sliced mushrooms and cook until they release their moisture and start to brown, about 5 minutes.
Add the frankfurters and cook, breaking them up with a spoon, until browned and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes.
Add the all-purpose flour and stir to combine with the browned frankfurters and mushrooms.
Gradually add the chicken broth, whisking continuously to avoid lumps, and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce heat to low and let the bangers and nimono simmer for 5-7 minutes, or until the potatoes are cooked through.
Mash the boiled potatoes with butter, milk, and heavy cream until smooth and creamy.
Serve the bangers and nimono over the creamy mash and enjoy!
